en.name: Venous blood specimen
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Venous blood specimen is a sample of blood obtained by venipuncture or from a venous catheter. It includes peripheral venous samples (routine biology), central venous samples (drawn from a central venous catheter) and mixed venous samples (drawn from a pulmonary artery catheter). Compared with arterial blood, venous blood has lower PO2 and SO2, higher PCO2, and slightly lower pH, reflecting tissue oxygen extraction and CO2 production. **OMOP CDM usage.** This concept set targets the `SPECIMEN.specimen_concept_id` field. In OMOP CDM v5.4, a MEASUREMENT row is linked to its SPECIMEN row via `measurement_event_id` (set to `specimen_id`) and `meas_event_field_concept_id` (a concept pointing to the SPECIMEN table); the older `FACT_RELATIONSHIP` table can also carry the link. In practice, many OMOP datasets do not materialise a SPECIMEN row and rely on the analyte-side LOINC (`pH of Venous blood`) to convey the specimen. This concept set is intended for datasets that explicitly model specimens. **Note: SNOMED does not provide a separate "Central venous blood specimen" code.** Central venous samples are recorded as `Venous blood specimen` at the specimen level; the central-venous distinction is captured by the analyte-side LOINC code (`... in Central venous blood`). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the SNOMED Specimen concept `Venous blood specimen`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to two specimens: - *4045667 / 122555007 — Venous blood specimen (SNOMED)* - *4001226 / 119298005 — Mixed venous blood specimen (SNOMED)* ## Excluded Concepts The following SNOMED Specimen descendant is deliberately excluded by name: - *45766302 / 703431007 — Venous cord blood specimen*: cord blood is physiologically distinct from post-natal venous blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default mapping** when the source data records a sample drawn from a peripheral or central vein: `4045667 / 122555007 — Venous blood specimen`. - **Source explicitly records a pulmonary artery catheter / Swan-Ganz sample**: map to `4001226 / 119298005 — Mixed venous blood specimen`. - **Source records umbilical / cord sampling**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Source records an arterial sample**: use "Arterial blood specimen" instead. ## References [1] SNOMED International. SNOMED CT — Venous blood specimen (122555007). Available via OHDSI Athena: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms/4045667
